transcript: Welcome to Behind the Ticker, the podcast where we go beyond the symbol and into the strategy. I'm Brad Roth, founder and chief investment officer at Thor Funds. And in each episode, I sit down with ETF managers, CIOS, and industry leaders to break down how these funds are actually built, how they behave in real markets, and how advisers use them in real portfolios. You know, it's funny because REITs are such a niche type industry. Um, when I was growing up, I grew up learning about Warren Buffett at a very very young age. Uh, I was my father put the Wall Street Journal in front of me when I was like six years old and I was fascinated by the stock tables and you know, they don't publish stock tables anymore. She said to me, "Kids your age don't get the opportunity that you're being given today." And it, you know, looking back on it, it kind of offended me, but I'd also realized how right she was because again, Dallas, Texas, you don't think of retrading desks and then, you know, huge Wall Street opportunities like a New York mindset. It was just a very different uh landscape than what we saw today. After Green Street went toward what I call a New York traders philosophy where it was sales traders, head trader executing trades and I lost the ability to basically push the buttons, which is what I always wanted to do. It was called Espazito Securities. Um, when I was at Espazito for several years, almost like my time at Green Street, I got to watch the ETF industry grow up. So many new issuers come out, so many new thematic type funds that came to the market. and to really be sitting, you know, in the action working with a lot of these new issuers that at the time, you know, weren't well known that today are household names in the industry. It was a fascinating business at the time and I really learned a lot. Um, got to a disagreement with the boss man uh last and then gave my resignation in 2018. This was back when the non-traded REITs were gaining redemptions and investors couldn't get out. We were trying to provide a rapper to allow investors to get into that. But a buddy of mine called me up and he's like, "Hey, I love what you do. You're so active on social. you're such a great storyteller. Why don't you keep doing that, but come do it for me? And you know, it really didn't take much twisting of my arm. When I joined up with Hoya in September of 23, we had around $75 million under management. But when we cap our waitings, our goal is and when I say cap, I mean like the biggest exposure is around one and a half% of a waiting. So, and our our hope is the majority of the REITs would raise the dividends throughout the year that would offset that company cutting their dividend. You know what other unique way I again correct me if I'm wrong. I still think we're the only REIT ETF that's on the market that combines both common and preferred stocks. So we have a 10% waiting or one-third of the 100 names in our portfolio that are liquid REIT preferred stocks. The REIT preferred market is a whole different animal on itself. And so the key is they have to be liquid because they have, you know, some of those have very wide spreads that you can lose that percent 2% very quickly. But that exposure to the preferred adds a couple of percent of yield to the bottom line. So I I had this conversation yesterday. Our focus really kind of plays like the NCAA basketball tournament. Last in first out type of situation.
